definition: Software as a Service — software delivered over the internet on a subscription basis.
extendedDefinition: SaaS eliminates the need for organizations to install and run applications on their own servers or devices. The provider manages all infrastructure, maintenance, and security, allowing customers to access the software via a web browser or API. This model has become the dominant delivery method for business software due to its lower upfront costs, automatic updates, and built-in scalability. Popular examples include Google Workspace, Salesforce, and Slack.
description: Software as a Service (SaaS) delivers software over the internet on a subscription basis. Learn how the SaaS model works and its benefits for businesses.
